message.content:
You are confirmer #2, independent. Confirm a production-hardening fix is BOTH appropriate (correct, no regression, idiomatic) AND complete (fully closes it; for a code change the named regression test must DISCRIMINATE — fail if the fix were reverted). Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay
Fix G-019 in src/assay_engine/orchestration/gates.py
What: Gate.evaluate type-guards the precondition return (non-GateDecision -> typed GateError).
Test(s): test_gate_rejects_non_gatedecision_precondition
Steps: read the actual changed file(s); read the test and judge whether it discriminates; check no regression. For a doc/refactor item, judge correctness/coverage rather than revert-discrimination. Verdict CONFIRMED (both true) or CONCERN (something missing/wrong/under-tested). Be skeptical and specific.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

65	    precondition: Callable[[Mapping[str, Any]], GateDecision]

67	    modes: frozenset[StudyMode] = ALL_MODES

69	    def evaluate(
70	        self,
71	        context: Mapping[str, Any],
72	        *,
73	        study_modes: frozenset[StudyMode] | None = None,
74	        record: ProvenanceRecorder | None = None,
75	    ) -> GateDecision:
76	        # The gate's own `modes` is a self-declaration. When the orchestration layer supplies
77	        # the running study's actual modes, the transition must be legal under THOSE modes —
78	        # so a discovery-only run cannot pass a CONFIRM->ADJUDICATE gate even if the gate
79	        # over-declares its modes (audit pass 2, issue #23). Fall back to self.modes only
80	        # until the runner is wired.
81	        effective_modes = self.modes if study_modes is None else (study_modes & self.modes)
82	        if not legal_transition(self.frm, self.to, effective_modes):
83	            raise GateError(

86	            )

90	        # instead (pass 4, #G-019 — the gate analogue of the #F-021 Verdict guards).
91	        if not isinstance(decision, GateDecision):
92	            raise GateError(
93	                f"gate {self.name!r} precondition returned {type(decision).__name__} — "
94	                "expected a GateDecision"
95	            )
96	        if not decision.approved:
97	            raise GateError(f"gate {self.name!r} blocked transition: {decision.reason}")
98	        if self.requires_human and record is None:
99	            # Fail loud: a human-in-the-loop gate must not pass without a recorder to
100	            # capture the approval to the (eventual) append-only provenance trail.
101	            raise GateError(
102	                f"gate {self.name!r} requires recorded human approval but no provenance "
103	                "recorder was supplied"
104	            )
105	        if record is not None:
106	            record(decision)
107	        return decision
108

content.text: The fix looks correct. Now let me read the test.

content.content:
85	def test_gate_rejects_non_gatedecision_precondition():
86	    # #G-019: a precondition returning a non-GateDecision (e.g. None — forgot to return) must raise
87	    # a typed GateError naming the contract, not an opaque AttributeError on decision.approved.
88	    g = Gate("g", Phase.INGEST, Phase.BASELINE, lambda ctx: None)
89	    with pytest.raises(GateError, match="expected a GateDecision"):
90	        g.evaluate({})
91	
92	
93	def test_requires_human_gate_fails_loud_without_recorder():
94	    # issue #11: a human-in-the-loop gate cannot pass without a provenance recorder
95	    g = Gate("g", Phase.INGEST, Phase.BASELINE, _approve, requires_human=True)
96	    with pytest.raises(GateError):
97	        g.evaluate({})
98	
99	
100	def test_requires_human_gate_records_when_recorder_supplied():
101	    recorded = []
102	    g = Gate("g", Phase.INGEST, Phase.BASELINE, _approve, requires_human=True)
103	    decision = g.evaluate({}, record=recorded.append)
104	    assert decision.approved
105	    assert recorded and recorded[0].gate == "g"
106	
107	
108	def test_gate_rejects_transition_illegal_for_running_study_modes():
109	    # issue #23: even a default-(ALL_MODES) gate must respect the running study's modes
110	    g = Gate("g", Phase.CONFIRM, Phase.ADJUDICATE, _approve)  # default modes
111	    assert g.evaluate({}).approved  # legal under the full pipeline
112	    with pytest.raises(GateError):
113	        g.evaluate({}, study_modes=DISCOVERY)  # discovery-only run forbids ADJUDICATE
114

content.text: Now let me verify the test passes, and confirm it discriminates (would fail if reverted). The test uses `Gate("g", Phase.INGEST, Phase.BASELINE, lambda ctx: None)` — I need to confirm INGEST->BASELINE is a legal transition so the test reaches the precondition guard (not failing earlier on legal_transition).

content.text: The discrimination is proven: reverted, it raises the exact opaque `AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'approved'` the fix is meant to prevent, and the test fails. Restored, but the git diff shows an unexpected change in `determinism.py`. Let me check that — I must ensure the working tree is clean.

input.command: cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && git diff && echo "=== status ===" && git status --short

content.text:
Working tree is now clean — the determinism.py diff was a transient artifact (likely a __pycache__/mtime-related stat from the test run); `git diff` and `git status` both report clean now. The gates.py file is fully restored.

Let me run the full orchestration test module to confirm no regression.

All checks pass. My assessment:

**Appropriate (correct, no regression, idiomatic):**
- The guard is placed correctly: after `decision = self.precondition(context)` and before `decision.approved` is accessed (gates.py:91-95).
- `isinstance(decision, GateDecision)` is the right check; raises a typed `GateError` (the module's domain error type) with a clear message naming the violated contract and the actual returned type.
- Consistent with the existing pattern in the file (the `legal_transition` and `requires_human` guards also raise `GateError`), and references the analogous `#F-021` Verdict guard.
- Full module (13 tests) passes — no regression.

**Complete (test discriminates):**
- `test_gate_rejects_non_gatedecision_precondition` uses `lambda ctx: None` as the precondition, and `INGEST→BASELINE` which is a legal transition under all modes, so execution reaches the precondition guard (not short-circuited by `legal_transition`).
- Passes with the fix; when I reverted the guard, the test FAILED with the exact `AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'approved'` that the fix prevents. The `match="expected a GateDecision"` assertion is specific to the new error message, not a generic catch.
- Working tree restored clean afterward.

input.reasoning: Fix verified in /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/orchestration/gates.py (lines 87-95). Gate.evaluate now type-guards the precondition return: after `decision = self.precondition(context)`, `if not isinstance(decision, GateDecision)` raises a typed GateError naming the violated contract and the actual returned type, instead of letting an opaque AttributeError surface at `decision.approved`. APPROPRIATE: correct placement (after the call, before .approved access), uses the module's domain error type, message is specific and informative, consistent with sibling guards (legal_transition, requires_human) and the referenced F-021 Verdict analogue; full orchestration module (13 tests) passes — no regression. COMPLETE/DISCRIMINATING: test_gate_rejects_non_gatedecision_precondition (tests/test_orchestration.py:85) uses lambda ctx: None over the legal INGEST->BASELINE transition so execution actually reaches the guard, and asserts pytest.raises(GateError, match="expected a GateDecision") — specific to the new message. It passes with the fix; reverting the guard makes it FAIL with exactly `AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'approved'`, proving discrimination. Working tree restored clean after the revert experiment.

content.text: Verified G-019: CONFIRMED. The fix in `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/orchestration/gates.py` is both appropriate and complete, and the test `test_gate_rejects_non_gatedecision_precondition` discriminates (fails with the exact opaque AttributeError when the guard is reverted).
